title: Recover from parse errors gracefully
description: Make the parser resynchronize after an error so it reports multiple useful diagnostics instead of one cascade of noise.
body: Use when building or improving a parser's error handling. Do not abort on the first syntax error, and do not emit a flood of bogus follow-on errors after the real one. Actions: implement panic-mode or phrase-level recovery (skip to a synchronizing token like ; or }), insert/delete a token where it clearly fixes the parse, and suppress cascaded errors caused by an already-reported failure. Validation signals include a test suite of malformed programs each yielding one accurate diagnostic and continued parsing. Risk boundary: poor recovery either hides real errors or buries them in noise, frustrating users; verify recovery on a corpus of broken programs rather than tuning it on one input.
